mechanism_narrative: A persistent downtrend in FEDFUNDS reflects the Federal Reserve's crisis-response easing cycle, which lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and reduces borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month as liquidity conditions loosen.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33 percent one month prior amid aggressive Fed easing following Lehman collapse
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX declines in more than 55% of months where FEDFUNDS falls month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly nonfarm payroll losses accelerate past a material threshold, forward-looking equity markets price in a deepening earnings recession, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as consensus growth expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 150,000 in the latest reported month, or UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over the trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS reported at 159218 thousand with deteriorating trend into Q4 2008
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase of recession
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of months following a triggered payroll-loss threshold, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 across a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below a critical low-activity threshold signals a deep contraction in residential investment and household balance sheets, which propagates through consumer wealth effects and construction employment to depress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1000 thousand units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1366 thousand units two months prior, near multi-decade lows
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns average positive in periods where HOUST remains below the 1000k threshold, or the relationship shows Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal Reserve rate cuts fail to stabilize equity markets when they occur simultaneously with accelerating unemployment, because the joint signal reveals policy is reactive to worsening fundamentals rather than proactively stimulative, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns despite falling FEDFUNDS.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over the trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Fed easing to 4.33 percent coinciding with rising unemployment signal in late 2008
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months meeting the joint trigger condition, or the interaction term shows no incremental Rank IC improvement over the single-series FEDFUNDS model.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production indicates contracting real economic activity and falling capacity utilization, which forward markets interpret as a deteriorating corporate earnings backdrop, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.511 two months prior, trending downward amid credit crunch
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Confirmation of two consecutive quarterly declines in real GDP establishes a recognized recession regime, in which equity risk premia remain structurally elevated and forward S&P 500 returns are negative over the subsequent quarter until growth data stabilizes.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 registers a quarter-over-quarter decline for two consecutive reported quarters
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23536.293 billion five months prior with growth momentum weakening into H2 2008
regime_hint: confirmed recession regime with negative GDP prints
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive on average during confirmed two-quarter GDP contraction periods, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in headline CPI toward disinflation or deflation signals collapsing aggregate demand and raises real debt burdens, which forward markets price as a negative shock to corporate revenue and equity valuations over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change turns negative or annualized inflation falls below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 319.775 one month prior with commodity prices collapsing sharply in Q4 2008
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of months following a CPI deceleration trigger, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based collapse in consumer demand across both discretionary spending and residential investment channels, amplifying the negative earnings revision cycle and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline exceeds 1% and HOUST year-over-year decline exceeds 20%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 723853 million and HOUST at 1366 thousand units both showing steep declines into late 2008
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following the joint trigger are not significantly more negative than returns following either single-series trigger alone (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.03.
